meta.description: Master catalog of Taranath Tantrik stories for read-only web UI

paragraphs: A fan-built index of Taranath Tantrik stories — original works from the Bandyopadhyay family and fan fiction from later writers and narrators. Browse, search, and filter the catalog, then jump straight to YouTube to listen.
This site is a read-only archive maintained by readers, not a publisher. It links out to publicly available audio and video rather than hosting story text.

character.heading: The character & original authors
paragraphs: Taranath Tantrik is a fictional Bengali occult practitioner who recounts supernatural adventures from his past. The character was created by Bibhutibhushan Bandyopadhyay, best known for Pather Panchali, in stories he wrote before his death in 1950. His son, Taradas Bandyopadhyay, continued the lineage — gathering, expanding, and writing new Taranath episodes that became widely known through print and radio.

title.en: Tarnath Tantrik-er Golpo

stories.notes: Foundational frame story; Taranath recounts his occult past to two young friends in Kolkata.

title.en: Madhusundari Debi'r Abirbhab

stories.notes: Taranath's encounter with the supernatural enchantress Madhusundari Debi near the Barakar river.

title.en: Matu Pagli

stories.notes: Taranath's first guru, the mysterious Matu Pagli of Birbhum; also adapted by Taradas for radio.

title.en: Subala

stories.notes: Original Subala episode from Bibhutibhushan's narrative. Taradas later wrote a separate Subala-related story (see Janmantar).

title.en: Jawrashur / Jwarasur

stories.notes: Childhood fever demon episode; expanded in Taradas's 1985 collection.

title.en: Betal

stories.notes: Taranath assigned to save a girl from a Kapalik.

title.en: Kalbhairav / Kaal Bhairav

stories.notes: Taranath earns Kalbhairav's attention but falters at a crucial moment.

title.en: Birjabala / Birajbala

stories.notes: Taranath senses a strange unseen presence after returning home.

title.en: Tarabhoirobi / Tarabhairavi

stories.notes: Young Taranath falls for an enchantress, triggering eerie events.

title.en: Bathtob

stories.notes: Taranath visits a rich man's home and senses a presence requiring exorcism; expanded by Taradas (Bishu Thakur episode).

title.en: Mukh

stories.notes: A face appears on the bathroom floor at Ramdulal's house.

title.en: Ranga Kakima

stories.notes: The story of Ranga Kakima and Birajbala from Taranath's family.

title.en: Brojobhushan-er Biswasprapti

stories.notes: Story of faith and the supernatural; broadcast in two parts on Sunday Suspense.

title.en: Janmantar

stories.notes: Reincarnation-themed Taranath story featuring Subala.

title.en: Agun
Jolpishach

stories.notes: Water-demon themed story.

title.en: Pasang Mara
Debdarshan

stories.notes: Often broadcast alongside Kaal Bhairav and Birjabhusan in classics.

title.en: Okhane Amangal
Bibhash Daakchhe
Amar Jiban
Panchamundi'r Asan
Tinti Kaak-er Golpo
Taranath Tantrik O Brahmapishach
Taranath Tantrik-er Aschorjyo Duniya
Alatchakra

stories.notes: Novel centered on Taranath; widely regarded as one of the finest works in the series.

title.en: Abar Taranath

stories.notes: Later collection continuing the Taranath series.

title.en: Taranather Protabartan (Bhondo Taranath)

stories.notes: Grandson of Bibhutibhushan; also published as Bhondo Taranath.

title.en: Taranather Bramhastra
Rudradeb Taranath
Kolkataye Taranath

stories.notes: Younger son of Taradas; audio story broadcast on Sunday Suspense.

title.en: Taranath Tantrik-er Golpo (Classics compilation)

stories.notes: Sunday Suspense classics episode combining Debdarshan, Kaal Bhairav and related narratives.

collections.title: Taranath Tantrik Samagra
collections.contents: Omnibus of Bibhutibhushan's 2 stories, Taradas's Taranath Tantrik (1985), Alatchakra, and later editions include additional Taradas stories.

adaptations.title: Taranath Tantrik (Hoichoi Web Series)
adaptations.notes: 10-episode web series based primarily on Bibhutibhushan's original stories.

adaptations.title: Abar Taranath
adaptations.author: Trijit Kar
adaptations.notes: Fan-fiction / homage by an unrelated author; not part of the Bandyopadhyay family canon.
